facts.context: What is the source of the material that causes meteor showers?
facts.truth: The nuclei of comets gradually disintegrate and spread out along the comet's orbital path. When the Earth passes through the orbit of a comet we are bombarded by sand-sized particles which cause a meteor shower.
distractors: Near-Earth asteroids gradually disintegrate and spread out along their orbital path. When the Earth passes through the orbit of an asteroid we are bombarded by sand-sized particles which cause a meteor shower.
Near-Earth asteroids disintegrate as they enter Earth's atmosphere creating hundreds of bright meteors that appear to radiate from a single location in the sky.
The nuclei of comets disintigrate as they enter Earth's atmosphere creating hundreds of bright meteors that appear to radiate from a central location in the sky.

facts.context: The Large Magellanic Cloud is ...
facts.truth: a dwarf galaxy orbiting the Milky Way.
distractors: the closest planetary nebula to the Earth.
a bright star cluster discovered by Magellan.
the outer arm of the Milky Way named after Magellan.

facts.context: On which planet in our solar system can you find the Great Red Spot?
facts.truth: Jupiter
distractors: Venus
Mars
Saturn

facts.context: Which of the following is/are common feature(s) of all fresh (i.e. not eroded) impact craters formed on solid surfaces:
facts.truth: A and B only
distractors: ejecta
raised rims
central peaks

facts.context: From laboratory measurements we know that a particular spectral line formed by hydrogen appears at a wavelength of 486.1 nanometers (nm). The spectrum of a particular star shows the same hydrogen line appearing at a wavelength of 485.9 nm. What can we conclude?
facts.truth: The star is moving toward us.
distractors: The star is getting hotter.
The star is getting colder.
The star is moving away from us.

facts.context: Why are the season's in Mars' southern hemisphere so extreme?
facts.truth: because Mars has a more eccentric orbit than the Earth
distractors: because Mars is farther from the sun than the Earth
because Mars has more carbon dioxide in its atmosphere than the Earth
because Mars' axis is more tilted than the Earth's

facts.context: Why are the inner planets made of denser materials than the outer planets?
facts.truth: In the inner part of the nebula only metals and rocks were able to condense because of the high temperatures whereas hydrogen compounds although more abundant were only able to condense in the cooler outer regions.
distractors: In the beginning when the protoplanetary disk was spinning faster centrifugal forces flung the lighter materials toward the outer parts of the solar nebula.
Denser materials were heavier and sank to the center of the nebula.

When the solar nebula formed a disk materials naturally segregated into bands and in our particular solar system the denser materials settled nearer the Sun while lighter materials are found in the outer part.

facts.context: What do meteorites reveal about the solar system?
facts.truth: They reveal that the age of the solar system is approximately 4.6 billion years.
distractors: They reveal that the early solar system consisted mostly of hydrogen and helium gas.
They reveal that meteorites are much older than the comets and planets.
They reveal that the solar system once contained 10 planets.

facts.context: What does the astronomical term ecliptic describe?
facts.truth: The path of the Sun in the sky throughout a year.
distractors: The axial tilt of the Earth throughout a year.
The movement of the stars due to Earth’s rotation.
The central line through the axis of rotation.

facts.context: Why is Saturn almost as big as Jupiter despite its smaller mass?
facts.truth: Jupiter's greater mass compresses it more thus increasing its density.
distractors: Saturn has a larger proportion of hydrogen and helium than Jupiter and is therefore less dense.
Jupiter's strong magnetic field constrains its size.
Saturn is further from the Sun thus cooler and therefore less compact.

facts.context: Which of the following moons is NOT one of the Galilean moons?
facts.truth: Dione
distractors: Ganymede
Callisto
Europa

facts.context: What about asteroids makes them stand out in sky surveys searching for them?
facts.truth: Asteroids have substantial motion relative to the background stars
distractors: Asteroids emit pulsed radiation
Asteroids emit a lot of their own radiation
They reflect enough of the sun's light to make them brighter than most background stars

facts.context: Which is not a similarity between Saturn and Jupiter's atmospheres?
facts.truth: an equatorial wind speed of more than 900 miles per hour
distractors: a composition dominated by hydrogen and helium
the presence of belts zones and storms
significant "shear" between bands of circulation at different latitudes

facts.context: The Pleiades is an open star cluster that plays a role in many ancient stories and is well-known for containing ... bright stars.
The nebular theory of the formation of the solar system successfully predicts all but one of the following. Which one does the theory not predict?
facts.truth: the equal number of terrestrial and jovian planets
distractors: Planets orbit around the Sun in nearly circular orbits in a flattened disk.
the craters on the Moon
asteroids Kuiper-belt comets and the Oort cloud

facts.context: A sand bag has a mass of 5kg and weight 50N on Earth. What is the mass and weight of this sand bag on a planet with half the gravity compared to Earth?
facts.truth: Mass 5kg weight 25N
distractors: Mass 5kg weight 100N
Mass 5kg weight 50N
Mass 10kg weight 100N

facts.context: Earth has been gradually warming over the past few decades. Based on a great deal of evidence scientists conclude that this warming is caused by ________.
facts.truth: human activities that are increasing the concentration of greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere
distractors: the fact that our politicians spout a lot of hot air
the human release of chemicals called CFCs into the stratosphere
the increase in forest fires during recent years

facts.context: The term Schwarzschild radius usually describes properties of ...
facts.truth: black holes.
distractors: red dwarfs.
pulsars.
galaxies.

facts.context: The two moons of Mars are called ...
facts.truth: Phobos and Deimos
distractors: Tritos and Desmos
Tritos and Deimos
Phobos and Tritos

facts.context: Why can't we see the Sun's corona except during total solar eclipses?
facts.truth: It's too diffuse
distractors: The corona is made up mostly of charged particles which aren't luminous.
It's much too cool to emit visible light
We can't see magnetic fields

facts.context: Why do we see essentially the same face of the Moon at all times?
facts.truth: because the Moon's rotational and orbital periods are equal
distractors: because the Moon has a nearly circular orbit around the Earth
because the Moon does not rotate
because the other face points toward us only at new moon when we can't see the Moon

facts.context: The second cosmic velocity (or escape velocity) is the speed required by an object to escape the gravitational field of a celestial body with mass M and radius R. Which formula correctly calculates this velocity? (G: gravitational constant)
facts.truth: v = sqrt(2GM/R)
distractors: v = sqrt(GM/R)
v = 2sqrt(GM/R)
v = sqrt(GM/2R)

facts.context: The star Betelgeuse is one of the brightest stars in the night sky with remarkable red color. In which constellation is Betelgeuse located?
facts.truth: Orion
distractors: Cassiopeia
Cygnus
Ursa Major

facts.context: Which of the following lists the ingredients of the solar nebula from highest to lowest percentage of mass in the nebula?
facts.truth: light gases (H He) hydrogen compounds (H2O CH4 NH3) rocks metals
distractors: hydrogen compounds (H2O CH4 NH3) light gases (H He) metals rocks
hydrogen compounds (H2O CH4 NH3) light gases (H He) rocks metals
light gases (H He) hydrogen compounds (H2O CH4 NH3) metals rocks

facts.context: Mars has an atmosphere that is almost entirely carbon dioxide.Why isn't there a strong greenhouse effect keeping the planet warm?
facts.truth: the atmosphere on Mars is too thin to trap a significant amount of heat
distractors: Mars does not have enough internal heat to drive the greenhouse effect
Mars is too far from the sun for the greenhouse effect to work
the greenhouse effect requires an ozone layer which Mars does not have

facts.context: Which one of these constellations is not located along the Milky Way in the sky?
facts.truth: Leo
distractors: Perseus
Cygnus
Scorpius

facts.context: From shortest to longest wavelength which of the following correctly orders the different categories of electromagnetic radiation?
facts.truth: gamma rays X rays ultraviolet visible light infrared radio
distractors: infrared visible light ultraviolet X rays gamma rays radio
radio infrared visible light ultraviolet X rays gamma rays
gamma rays X rays visible light ultraviolet infrared radio

facts.context: Which of the jovian planets have rings?
facts.truth: all of the above
distractors: Neptune
Uranus
Saturn

facts.context: Besides large astronomical objects astrophysicists are also interested in small particles from space. Which one of these particles has the weakest interactions with other particles?
facts.truth: ν Neutrinos
distractors: µ Muons
Antiparticles
π Pions

facts.context: Find the best approximation for the surface temperature of the Sun:
